This might affect how transitions between time zones or offsets are handled.

Now, looking at the Failing Tests Information. There's a status change for several tests. There are also several tests that went from passing to failing. For instance, TestDateTimeZone#testIsLocalDateTimeGap_Berlin and others started failing after this mutation. These were previously working but now they're broken, which suggests that the mutant might have introduced some side effects or bugs in those areas.

So, considering all these changes, I need to assess whether this single mutation affects only the adjacent mutants (i.e., nearby code) or if it has broader implications. The fact that multiple unrelated tests started failing after applying this mutation suggests that the change isn't isolated. It might be affecting various parts of the system that rely on correct time zone calculations.

Therefore, the mutant's impact is not limited to just adjacent lines; it seems to have ripple effects across different test cases and functionalities. This implies that it's likely influencing non-adjacent mutants or related components.
